explicit_text: I'm Davey Day, the fast-stepping New boy on his way up in boxing. I've fought many fighters around New York City, including Frankie Segni, Roger Bernard, and Bobby Paoli, and I only dropped one pro fight in 1931. I recently fought Henry Armstrong and although I lost, I was handicapped by a swollen eye. I'm determined to become the champ and keep the title right here in my hometown.

explicit_text: Henry Armstrong was a tough opponent I faced in the ring. He was a smart and determined fighter who never quit swinging, and he gave me a hard time especially in the twelfth round when my eye was swollen shut. Armstrong had a habit of muttering to himself between rounds, which some say means he's getting punchy, but I think he's just confident. After our fight, he even came to my hotel to apologize for swelling my eye, showing he's an intelligent man who reads books.

explicit_text: I fought Lou Ambers in New York when he was the champ, and although I beat many fighters around town, Ambers was a tough one. Armstrong also fought Ambers and took a lot of punishment, including a badly cut lip that needed fourteen stitches. Ambers was given the title despite his injuries, which I think wouldn't have happened if he were white. Ambers was a significant figure in the boxing scene during my career.

explicit_text: A dame once wrote me a fan letter saying I looked like George Raft, who used to be a pug too. I don’t think I look like him, but I guess someone must have thought I was punchy already. George Raft was a boxer before he became famous, and it’s funny to be compared to him.
